prompt: Photorealistic turntable sprite sheet for a wine education website: twelve sequential views of the same Carmenere grape cluster rotating around its vertical stem axis, arranged in exactly 3 columns by 4 rows. Each panel must contain one complete grape cluster with generous empty background margin on all four sides. The cluster must remain centered, similarly sized, and fully visible in every panel; no berry, stem, shadow, leaf, or object may touch the cell edge. Show medium blue-black berries, moderately loose cluster, ripe dark skins, green-brown stems, subtle savory Bordeaux-family character. Use consistent dark teal studio-vineyard background, consistent natural side lighting, realistic grape morphology, authentic wine-grape scale, subtle dusty bloom, and a museum-quality macro photography look. This must be useful for a drag-to-rotate object viewer, so each panel should represent a distinct rotation angle around the same bunch. No text, no labels, no watermark, no numbers, no logos, no hands, no bottle, no glass, no cartoon style.
use_case: photorealistic grape-cluster turntable sprite sheet for interactive wine education
style: macro studio photography, realistic wine grape morphology, consistent lighting, generous photo margins
composition: portrait 3 by 4 grid, twelve clean full-object turntable frames, each grape cluster centered with wide empty margin and no cropping
constraints: no text, no labels, no watermark, no panel numbers, no visible grid lines required, no clipped top, no clipped bottom, no repeated fragments, no people, no bottle, no glass, no illustration, no table, no shelf, no pedestal, no surface line, no horizon line, no reflection stripe, no bright horizontal streak near bottom, empty continuous background all the way to bottom edge
